title: Networking Equipment Selector — Switch Knowledge Base Schema (C9500)
description: Series-agnostic shape + authoring contract for a switch knowledge base. This schema enforces SHAPE (entries are well-formed); switching-axes.json enforces MEANING (which axes exist, their KIND/ROLE and legal enum values); selector/tools/validate-kb.mjs enforces CROSS-REFERENCE INTEGRITY (group references resolve, valid_primary is a subset of the referenced PSU group, poe_budget_watts equals the matrix max, sum of access-port counts equals total_port_count, enum/speed values are registered). All three must pass. See $authoring for the procedure an agent follows to add a model. This copy adds TWO structural extensions over the base 0.8.0 shape: (1) a fan_trays configurable (catalog.fan_trays / groups.fan_tray_groups / configurables.fan_trays), needed because Catalyst 9500X airflow direction (front-to-back vs back-to-front) is a real order-time SKU choice — no prior series had a selectable fan category; (2) a model-level access_pair_block, for a homogeneous model whose ACCESS ports are a bank of combinable pairs (C9500-32QC: 32 QSFP+ ports = 16 pairs, each configured to 2x40G OR 1x100G). It is a pure port-capability/feasibility concept resolved at solve time — it never appears in the BOM (you don't order access ports), so it does NOT ride through the uplink/orderable-option channel. To allow it, model.ports minItems was relaxed 1->0 (a model may carry its whole port bank in access_pair_block instead of static ports rows); validate-kb.mjs requires ports non-empty OR access_pair_block present.

$authoring._comment: NON-VALIDATING. Procedure + rules for an agent populating new switch models. Read this before touching data.
$authoring.prime_directive: Push everything into DATA; keep the solver small and dumb. Never encode a compatibility quirk as logic. If a relationship between specs exists (speed sets, PSU-pair budgets, tier locks), express it as data in this KB, not as code elsewhere.
sourcing_discipline: All facts come from authoritative Cisco documentation ONLY — never from memory or inference.
Derive the fetch targets from the KB header 'sources' block (datasheet URL + ordering guide URL).
Fetch the datasheet AND the ordering guide before proposing any values for a model.
Record how each resolved value was derived in the model's axis_value_provenance block, citing the source table.
$authoring.working_copy_discipline: Project knowledge files are the source of truth; edit them in lockstep (registry -> schema -> KB) and run validate-kb.mjs before considering a change done. When catalog/group state becomes uncertain mid-session, rebuild deterministically from an explicit list rather than patching incrementally.

cheap_do_freely: Add a new catalog SKU (network module, PSU, cable, license, fan tray).
Add a new group, even one whose membership duplicates an existing group.
structural_STOP_and_ask: Adding, renaming, or re-typing an AXIS in switching-axes.json (touches UI + MCP params + maybe solver). Bump registry_version only after human sign-off.
Adding a new legal enum value to an existing axis (incl. a new port_speed / port_medium / port_role).
Changing the KIND or ROLE of an axis.
Any change to this schema's required-field or conditional rules.

axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.rule: Sort every datasheet property three ways before deciding where it goes.
axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.axis: A filterable capability. Declared in switching-axes.json (with KIND + ROLE) and supplied as a value under model.axis_values, OR (for ports) as structured rows under model.ports addressed by the parametrised port_count axis. Example: poe_type, total_port_count, port_count{role,medium,speed}, stacking_capable.
axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.variable: The orderable option that REALIZES a capability, picked AFTER a switch survives the filter. Lives inside model.configurables (a group reference or inline block). NOT an axis. Example: the specific PSU pair, the fitted network module, the subscription term length, the C9500X fan airflow direction.
axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.attribute: Displayed, never filtered, never picked. Lives loose under model.attributes. Example: switching_capacity_gbps, weight, MTBF.

capability_resolution.ports: Port capability is the unified DATA model (registry v0.4.0). model.ports is an array of port groups {count, role, medium, speeds[]}: N physical ports each able to run a SET of speeds. ACCESS ports (role=access) live on the model. UPLINK ports come from the fitted network module (catalog network_module.ports, role=uplink) on modular models (uplink_modular=true), or are carried inline on the model as role=uplink rows on fixed-uplink models (uplink_modular=false). A configured VARIANT's port pool = model access groups + the fitted module's uplink groups. The parametrised port_count axis counts ports in matching groups whose speeds[] include the requested speed.
capability_resolution.speed_subsumption: Subsumption is DATA: a port lists every speed it natively supports (a 25G SFP28 access/uplink port -> [1g,10g,25g]; a 10G mGig copper port -> [1g,2.5g,5g,10g]; a QSFP28 100G port -> [40g,100g]). The solver NEVER infers downshift. QSFP/SFP BREAKOUT (e.g. 40G->4x10G) is deliberately NOT modelled here: it needs a breakout cable SKU and is a future configurable, not a free speed.
capability_resolution.poe_budget: poe_budget_watts is the MAX across configurables.power_supplies.poe_budget_matrix ({primary, secondary} -> watts). The PSU pair delivering a requested wattage is read from the matrix at solve time. C9500 is not PoE-capable at all: every model sets poe_capable=false, poe_type='none', and an empty poe_budget_matrix.
capability_resolution.totals: total_port_count = sum of model.ports[role=access] counts (validator-checked). It does NOT count uplinks.
capability_resolution.fan_trays: NEW for C9500. Most models have a fixed, non-selectable fan tray (recorded as included_by_default_non_selectable.fan_tray, a plain descriptive string — NOT a configurables reference, since there is nothing to pick). Only Catalyst 9500X models have a real order-time choice (airflow direction, front-to-back vs back-to-front): those carry configurables.fan_trays {group, valid_options, default_option} referencing groups.fan_tray_groups, mirroring the shape of configurable_power_supplies (group + valid subset + default) but without a PoE-style matrix, since a fan tray has no capacity dimension — just a direction.

per_model_procedure: 1. Read the KB header 'sources'; fetch datasheet + ordering guide for the target model.
2. For every property the datasheet exposes, apply axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute. If it implies a NEW axis / enum value / port_speed -> STOP, surface for ruling.

3. Fill axis_values with the registry scalar axes (omit optional axes that don't apply). Build model.ports as access port groups (+ inline uplink groups for fixed-uplink models). Never put port counts in axis_values.

4. Build configurables: reference existing groups where the set matches; create a new group if the set differs. Build the poe_budget_matrix from the datasheet PSU table; set poe_budget_watts = matrix max. For C9500X, also set configurables.fan_trays; for every other C9500 model, record the fixed fan tray under included_by_default_non_selectable instead.
5. Fill axis_value_provenance for every resolved value, citing the source table.
7. Validate: this schema (shape) + validate-kb.mjs (cross-reference integrity). Both must pass.
conventions: A homogeneous model whose ACCESS ports are a bank of combinable pairs (each pair configured 2x-low OR 1x-high, e.g. C9500-32QC's 16 QSFP+ pairs at 2x40G|1x100G) uses model.access_pair_block, NOT static role=access ports rows. It is a port-CAPABILITY concept only: resolve.js accessConfigs() expands it into pairs+1 real simultaneous configurations at solve time, and it NEVER appears in the BOM (access ports aren't separately ordered) — so it must not be routed through uplinkOptions/the orderable-option channel. Set model.ports to [] (the bank IS the pair block), keep no_uplink_ports=true if there's no uplink subset (orthogonal — that flag is about uplinks), and total_port_count = static access sum + pairs*low.ports_per_pair (validator-checked). Combining is a static admin choice with NO speed fallback unless the datasheet/operator confirms one (32QC: high={1x[100g]}, no 40g).
License groups are split on BOTH port-class and tier. Tier is locked by the -E/-A hardware suffix; the subscription tier must match the network-stack tier.
Term length (3/5/7-yr) is a VARIABLE, not an axis — it lives in the license group's term_variable.
PSU groups represent the COMPLETE orderable family for a series, not just what one switch's PoE matrix needs.
Fan tray groups follow the same convention: a group is the complete set of orderable fan-tray SKUs for a sub-line; per-model valid options / default live on the model's configurables.fan_trays.
Series-specific groups (PSU, stacking cables, fan trays) are kept as their own named groups even when membership currently matches another series, so they can diverge later.
Ports: declare the real per-port speed SET; the implicit '1G-copper remainder' is now an explicit role=access, medium=copper, speeds=[1g] group, so no inference is needed.

PSU redundancy is NOT an axis: it is realised by a (primary, secondary) pair with secondary != none. configurables.power_supplies.default_primary is the single PSU the switch ships with (Cisco default-PSU table). The configurator starts from default_primary and, only when a PoE request exceeds it, ADDS a secondary (from the PoE matrix) before upsizing the primary; psu_redundancy=true forces a pair. NOTE for C9500: Cisco's own CCW ordering flow defaults to a redundant (primary+secondary) pair and requires actively selecting C9500-SPS-NONE to decline it — a different default posture than the generic policy assumes (which only adds a secondary when a PoE request exceeds the primary, and C9500 has no PoE demand at all). This is flagged for Pass-2 model authoring, not resolved here.

License TIER is a FILTER axis (license_tier = the SET of tiers a model offers, derived from its license groups; mirrors license_regime). Choosing a tier drops models that cannot provide it (DNA -E vs -A). License TERM stays a config-variable that resolves the concrete subscription SKU from subscription_members via the '-<n>Y' suffix.

StackWise Virtual (SVL) is Advantage-tier only (datasheet Table 24: 'High availability and resiliency' — StackWise Virtual is a Network Advantage feature, absent from Network Essentials). Every C9500/C9500X -A (Advantage) model sets stacking_capable=true, stacking_technology='stackwise-virtual', and carries NO configurables.stack_cables (SVL has no cable SKU — it runs over ordinary uplink ports). Every -E (Essentials) model sets stacking_capable=false.

The Cisco Catalyst Software Subscription ('unified' regime, C9500-DNX-* SKUs) is deliberately NOT modeled for C9500 — only the legacy 'dna-term' regime (C9500-DNA-*) is in scope, per project ruling. Do not add C9500-DNX-* catalog/license entries without new sign-off.

C9500-12Q/24Q/40X (removed per the datasheet's own document history, Dec 2024) and C9500-16X plus its two network modules C9500-NM-8X/C9500-NM-2Q (removed per a separate Cisco End-of-Sale/End-of-Life bulletin) are NOT modeled. Their associated catalog items exclusive to those models (e.g. PWR-C4-950WAC-R/950WDC-R, FAN-T4-R, the two network modules) are also excluded — do not add them without new sourcing.
The multi-unit bundle SKU (C9500-48Y4C-A-BUN) and the RFID opt-in/out SKUs (C9500-RFID / C9500X-RFID / C9500-RFID-NONE) are out of scope, same footing as pricing — do not model them.

models.description: Switch entries. Each is a flat, self-contained model. No inheritance between sibling variants (-E vs -A).

port_group.description: A homogeneous group of N physical ports, each able to run the SAME set of speeds. The unit of the v0.4.0 port model. role=access groups live on a model; role=uplink groups live on a catalog network_module (modular) or inline on a fixed-uplink model.

speeds.$comment: Ordered low->high per switching-axes.json port_speed (registry v0.9.0 added 50g). Every speed the port natively supports (subsumption as data). 100M is display-only and not listed.

header.description: Provenance + versioning for the whole KB.

catalog.description: Atomic SKU facts: one row per real orderable component.

catalog_network_module.description: A network (uplink) module. Carries EITHER a single fixed `ports` config OR a `modes` array of mutually-exclusive alternative port configs. A module with `modes` is still ONE orderable SKU: the fitted variant selects exactly one mode, and the kitlist/BOM shows the real module id with a mode label. A mode is NEVER a separate SKU.

ports.$comment: Single fixed config: role=uplink port groups this module provides, combined into a variant's port pool when fitted. Mutually exclusive with `modes`.

modes.$comment: Mutually-exclusive alternative port configs of ONE module. The fitted variant uses exactly one mode. Mutually exclusive with `ports`.

catalog_fan_tray.description: NEW for C9500. A physical fan tray SKU. Recorded for every real fan-tray part (even where a model treats it as fixed/non-selectable), so the atomic-SKU-fact convention holds; only models with a genuine airflow-direction choice (C9500X) reference one via a configurables.fan_trays group.

groups.description: Named shared compatibility SETS. A switch references a group by name and inherits the whole set.

network_module_group.description: The set of network modules compatible with a model. Per-module port capability lives on each catalog network_module (ports[]); the variant materialiser fits bay_count modules from members.

power_supply_group.description: Complete orderable PSU family for a series. Which member is a valid PRIMARY and the PoE budget per pair are switch-specific and live on the model.

fan_tray_group.description: NEW for C9500. Complete orderable fan-tray family for a sub-line (currently just the C9500X airflow-direction pair). Which options are valid and the default live on the model's configurables.fan_trays. No 'none' option: a fan tray is always present, never removable.

license_group.description: A license SET keyed on regime + tier + port_class. A model links to the set, not a single license.

model.description: One switch SKU. Flat and self-contained.

ports.$comment: Port groups for THIS switch: role=access always; role=uplink only on fixed-uplink models (uplink_modular=false). Modular models carry access only; uplink groups come from the fitted module. May be EMPTY only when access_pair_block carries the whole port bank instead (C9500-32QC); validate-kb.mjs requires ports non-empty OR access_pair_block present.

_incomplete.$comment: Names of fields whose values are NOT yet sourced from authoritative docs. validate-kb.mjs downgrades checks on these fields to WARNINGS for this model. Tracked centrally in DB/switching/THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md. Remove an entry once the field is confirmed.

no_uplink_ports.$comment: NEW for C9500. Opt-in escape hatch for a fixed-uplink model (uplink_modular=false) that genuinely has no dedicated uplink ports — e.g. C9500-32C/32QC are homogeneous 32-port switches with no Cisco-labeled 'uplink' subset. When true, validate-kb.mjs skips its usual 'fixed-uplink model must carry role=uplink port rows' check for this model, AND requires that the model carry zero role=uplink rows (catches accidental misuse). Absent/false everywhere else — does not change behavior for any other series or for C9500 models that do have a real uplink split (48Y4C/24Y4C/28C8D/60L4D). Orthogonal to access_pair_block: no_uplink_ports is about UPLINKS (there are none), access_pair_block is about the ACCESS bank (it's combinable) — C9500-32QC honestly declares both.

access_pair_block.$comment: NEW for C9500. A homogeneous ACCESS bank of combinable pairs (C9500-32QC: 16 pairs, each 2x40G OR 1x100G). This is a port-CAPABILITY concept only — it is resolved at solve time into pairs+1 real simultaneous access-port configurations (a query survives if any configuration satisfies it) and NEVER appears in the BOM (access ports are inherent to the SKU, not separately ordered). It does not touch the uplink/orderable-option channel. When present, model.ports typically carries no static role=access rows for the same bank (the pair block IS the bank); total_port_count = static access sum + pairs*low.ports_per_pair (validator-checked).

access_pair_block.description: A bank of N combinable ACCESS port pairs. Each pair operates as EITHER low.ports_per_pair ports at the low speeds OR high.ports_per_pair ports at the high speeds — combining a pair to the high speed is a static per-port admin configuration that consumes the whole pair (no dynamic speed negotiation, no fallback). Example: C9500-32QC 32 QSFP+ ports = 16 pairs, low {2x[40g]}, high {1x[100g]}. Not itself a port_group; resolve.js accessConfigs() materialises the pairs+1 discrete configurations (k = 0..pairs pairs in high mode) at solve time.

pair_side.description: One operating mode of every pair in an access_pair_block: `ports_per_pair` role=access ports, each able to run the SAME `speeds` set on the given `medium`.

axis_values.description: Scalar registry-axis values for this switch. additionalProperties is FALSE: only declared scalar axes may appear. Port counts are NOT here (they live in model.ports, addressed by the parametrised port_count axis). Optional axes are omitted when they do not apply.

license_tier.description: SET of license tiers the model offers (enum-set), derived from its license groups. Filter axis, parallel to license_regime.

configurables.description: Thin references to shared groups + switch-specific overlay. network_modules is present iff axis_values.uplink_modular is true. fan_trays is present iff the model has a genuine airflow-direction choice (currently: C9500X only). Cross-field consistency enforced by validate-kb.mjs.

default_primary.$comment: The single PSU the switch ships with by default. Must be a member of valid_primary (validator-checked).

configurable_fan_trays.description: NEW for C9500. Present only on models with a genuine airflow-direction choice (C9500X). Mirrors configurable_power_supplies' group + valid-subset + default shape, without a capacity matrix (a fan tray has no capacity dimension, just a direction).

default_option.$comment: Must be a member of valid_options (validator-checked convention, mirrors default_primary).
